reason: While the change is a configuration edit, it involves the bootloader (GRUB2). Incorrectly configuring the superuser or failing to regenerate the grub.cfg correctly can lead to a system that is unbootable or prevents administrative access to boot-time parameters. Because it touches the bootloader, it carries a risk of system breakage if the syntax is incorrect.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for bootloader misconfiguration; Risk of losing access to boot-time kernel parameters if superuser credentials are lost or incorrectly set; Requires successful execution of grub2-mkconfig to apply changes
